tool: sep 1.4.1 (SExtractor core, pip — same packaging precedent as the pip astrometry engine) vs the in-house peak-centroid detector; identical astrometry.net solver + cached 4200 indexes (scales 13-19), blind, sip_order 3; Siril 1.4.4 spcc for the end-to-end check
purpose: A/B for the solve_field.detect_stars removal condition: does an official extractor return trailed sources and measure at least as well? (BACKLOG item 7; the image2xy binary variant needs `apt install astrometry.net` and stays open as a cross-check.)

extraction.note: trailed sources ARE returned (no roundness gate), matching the source-verification in the dead-end registry; 20-sigma over sep's background model, flux-ranked, 25 px de-crowding, brightest 200 — the same post-processing the peaks detector applies

solve.center_delta_note: the documented SIP wobble at wide index scales (dead-end registry: 65 px MEDIAN between two solves of the same sensor) — the linear solve is what the consumer uses

end_to_end.check: inject the sep-derived WCS -> siril spcc -catalog=localgaia (sensor-null), tag sepab
end_to_end.vs_recorded_peaks_run: IDENTICAL K to all three decimals (recorded: 1.000/0.713/0.896); photometry 3058/5210 kept vs 3056/5210 (2-star border-reject difference, 0.07%); b_offsets agree to <0.1%
end_to_end.conclusion: SPCC re-matches stars from the WCS seed itself, so the SIP-level center wobble does not reach the consumer

verdict: REMOVAL CONDITION FIRED: an official extractor (SExtractor core) returns trailed sources, solves BETTER (logodds 299.5 vs 288.6), and measures at least as well end-to-end (identical SPCC K). solve_field.py default switched to --detect=sep; the in-house peaks path retained as --detect=peaks fallback until the x86 day-1 solve passes on sep, then delete.
